channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Crimson Desert developers "doing our utmost" to respond to its various launch issues, as game still passes huge one-day sales milestone
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert - the ambitious single player RPG spin-off from Pearl Abyss - has sold through 2m copies following its release last night.

The studio shared news of the milestone this afternoon, saying it has been "humbled" by the reception, while simultaneously acknowledging the game's rough edges at launch.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

"Thank you so much to our fans, community, and everyone who has joined us in Pywel," the Crimson Desert team wrote on social media.

"We will listen closely to the wide range of feedback shared by the community and work to make improvements quickly, doing our utmost to make the journey ahead even more enjoyable for our players."

While Pearl Abyss has not specified which issues it will be seeking to improve, complaints have ranged from those who bought the game via the Xbox App only to discover the game just wouldn't launch on their PCs, to poor performance on a base PS5 model and repeated assets. Some, meanwhile, have found the controls for Crimson Desert especially troublesome, and have already asked for a refund.

There is a lot of love for the cats in the game, though!

"A vast world and even vaster array of MMO-like activities mix with glittering fidelity in Crimson Desert, but what good is it without much character, texture or charm?" asks Eurogamer's own Crimson Desert review.

If you are one of the many who are currently playing Crimson Desert yourself, be sure to check out our various guides. Here's how to tame horses, how to steal and how to fish to get you started.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/crimson-desert-sales-milestone-launch-day-issues-fix
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Psychonauts and Keeper developer's Game Pass party game Kiln gets a release date, and it's coming really very soon
item.content:encoded:
Including on PC and ClayStation.

Double Fine - the studio behind the likes of Psychonauts and last year's wonderfully surreal lighthouse adventure Keeper - has announced a release date for its next quirky offering, known as Kiln.

Kiln is set to arrive next month, on 23rd April, across Xbox Series X/S, Xbox on PC, Xbox Cloud, PlayStation 5 and Steam. It will also be available day one via Xbox Game Pass Ultimate.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

Kiln was first announced back in January of this year, and unlike the studio's recent offerings, this time Double Fine is setting its sights on the multiplayer online party brawler genre, but with a pottery-themed twist. "Create your pot, grab your friends, and join a team of colourful spirits who face off against one another in online arenas, where the ceramic creations you sculpt on a pottery wheel become the bodies you bring into battle," reads Kiln's official blurb.

"The shape of your pots affect your attributes and your play-style. Will you be big, or small? Wide, or tall? Will you make a plate, a jug, a cup or a bowl? There are a multitude of different sizes and combinations to discover, each with unique abilities and attacks, along with new and surprising ways to play."

Before Kiln's release, there will be the chance to take part in playtests, with an open beta on Steam scheduled for between 9th and 11th April. If this sounds like your thing, you can sign up via Double Fine here. Or, if you are still unsure, you can check out a trailer for Kiln below.

I've always had a soft spot for Double Fine, and was especially enamored with Keeper, which I completed in one sitting last year. Our Matt was similarly smitten, calling it a "surprising, shapeshifting adventure of captivating wonder and beauty" in Eurogamer's five star Keeper review.

Kiln

PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/psychonauts-and-keeper-developers-game-pass-party-game-kiln-gets-a-release-date-and-its-coming-really-very-soon
